user.title: Energy sources
user.description: Point the brain at the sensors that describe your home's energy. Only the grid meter is required — add PV, battery and price for solar- and price-aware charging.

data_description.grid_power_entity: Your main meter's power. Positive = drawing from the grid, negative = exporting. This is how the brain detects surplus.
data_description.pv_power_entity: Your solar inverter's current production. Enables charging from solar surplus.
data_description.battery_soc_entity: Home battery charge level in %. Lets the brain keep a reserve before sending power to the car.
data_description.battery_power_entity: Home battery charge/discharge power. Helps tell battery flow apart from real grid surplus.
data_description.battery_hold_entity: A switch or input_boolean that, when on, stops your home battery from discharging (e.g. a Victron 'disable discharge' helper). The brain turns it on whenever it deliberately charges from the grid (cheap hours, the departure plan, Fast) so the car draws from the grid, never the battery. During solar charging it stays off, so the battery can buffer surplus fluctuations.
data_description.price_entity: Sensor with the current electricity price (e.g. Tibber, EPEX). Enables price-aware charging.
data_description.price_forecast_attr: Leave blank — auto-detected from your price sensor (Nordpool, EPEX Spot, EnergyZero and others). Only set this to override detection (the attribute name holding upcoming hourly prices).

charger.title: go-e charger
charger.description: Select the entities the brain uses to read and control your go-e wallbox.

data_description.goe_current_number: The number entity that sets the charging current (A). The brain writes to this to steer charging.
data_description.goe_phase_entity: Entity that switches between 1 and 3 phases, if your charger supports it. Leave empty to keep phases fixed.
data_description.goe_force_entity: The go-e force-state entity ('Neutral / Don't charge / Charge', frc). The brain uses this to actually start and stop charging — without it the charger can't go below its 6 A minimum, so pausing won't work.
data_description.goe_connected_entity: Tells the brain whether a car is plugged in. Charging only starts when a car is connected.
data_description.goe_power_entity: The charger's actual delivered power. Used for feedback and statistics.
data_description.goe_energy_entity: The charger's energy delivered in the current session (go-e 'energy since car connected'). Shown live next to the running session on the card.
data_description.voltage: Mains voltage per phase (≈230 V in the EU). Used to convert between amps and watts.
data_description.phases: How many phases charging uses by default when automatic phase switching is off.

steve.title: SteVe (optional)
steve.description: Connect to your SteVe OCPP server to read charging sessions and per-RFID energy, and to authorize/block tags or start/stop charging remotely. Leave the URL empty to skip — the brain works without it.

data_description.steve_url: Base URL of your SteVe server, e.g. http://192.168.1.10:8080. Enter only the host — the path is added automatically.
data_description.steve_username: The SteVe web user (default 'admin' — your auth.user in main.properties), not the OCPP tag owner.
data_description.steve_api_password: The web user's API password — i.e. your 'webapi.value' (web-api-secret) from SteVe's main.properties, not the login password.
data_description.steve_charge_box_id: Default charge box used by the remote start/stop services. Leave empty to always pass it explicitly.

init.title: Re-map entities
init.description: Change any of the sensors or charger entities the brain uses.

authorize_tag.name: Authorize RFID tag
authorize_tag.description: Allow an RFID id-tag to start charging in SteVe.

id_tag.name: ID tag
id_tag.description: The OCPP id-tag (RFID card) to authorize. Defaults to the tag chosen in the 'Selected tag' dropdown.

entry_id.name: Integration entry
entry_id.description: Only needed when more than one SteVe connection is configured.

block_tag.name: Block RFID tag
block_tag.description: Prevent an RFID id-tag from starting charging in SteVe.

id_tag.name: ID tag
id_tag.description: The OCPP id-tag (RFID card) to block. Defaults to the tag chosen in the 'Selected tag' dropdown.

entry_id.name: Integration entry
entry_id.description: Only needed when more than one SteVe connection is configured.

set_tag_name.name: Name RFID tag
set_tag_name.description: Give an RFID id-tag a friendly name (stored as its SteVe note) so it's recognizable everywhere instead of a raw UID.

id_tag.name: ID tag
id_tag.description: The OCPP id-tag (RFID card) to name.

name.name: Name
name.description: Friendly name, e.g. the user or car. Leave empty to clear the name and revert to the raw id-tag.

entry_id.name: Integration entry
entry_id.description: Only needed when more than one SteVe connection is configured.

remote_start.name: Remote start charging
remote_start.description: Ask SteVe to start a transaction on a charge point.

id_tag.name: ID tag
id_tag.description: The OCPP id-tag to charge under. Defaults to the tag chosen in the 'Selected tag' dropdown.

charge_box_id.name: Charge box ID
charge_box_id.description: Target charge point. Defaults to the one configured in options.

entry_id.name: Integration entry
entry_id.description: Only needed when more than one SteVe connection is configured.

remote_stop.name: Remote stop charging
remote_stop.description: Ask SteVe to stop a running transaction.

transaction_id.name: Transaction ID
transaction_id.description: Which session to stop. Defaults to the single active session if omitted.

charge_box_id.name: Charge box ID
charge_box_id.description: Target charge point. Defaults to the configured / active one.

entry_id.name: Integration entry
entry_id.description: Only needed when more than one SteVe connection is configured.
